site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 6 wrap for tipping results, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 6 tips, betting notes, and Mark Hughes Foundation charity tracking.

ticker: round 6 finished with the board going 4 from 8 on tips
cowboys h2h landed again and the charity record improved to 2 and 0
charity profit now sits at $11.50 from two settled wins
warriors smashing storm and titans torching parra were the big round-shifters
tigers beat the knights 42-22 and keep looking like the real surprise packet
bank the wins, learn from the misses, and reset cleanly for round 7

featurePanels.title: Round Review
items: Tigers backed up the hype with a 42-22 win over Newcastle
Warriors belted Storm 38-14 and changed the power map
Titans hammering the Eels 52-10 was the result nobody could ignore
The charity process stayed disciplined even when the tips split

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Tigers are no longer just a novelty story
Warriors suddenly look like a serious top-end side
Storm need a response after four straight losses
Eels and Dragons are slipping into danger territory fast

betCard.title: Round 6 Charity Bet Result
why: Cowboys come in off back-to-back wins including a 32-0 result.
Walsh being out changes Brisbane's attacking shape.
The price is longer than the form line suggests.
risks: Broncos have a strong home and head-to-head record in this matchup.
The attached snapshot mentions Reynolds uncertainty, which should be re-checked.
Derby games can flip quickly on field position and discipline.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.

watchlist: Tigers have turned surprise energy into a real ladder push.
Warriors just made a loud statement against Melbourne.
Storm and Eels both need to earn trust back next week.
Dragons keep sliding and are now firmly in pressure territory.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 6 wrapped with a split tipping card
lines: Round 6 finished at four correct tips from eight, with the board splitting cleanly between hits and misses.
The big positive was the Cowboys charity play landing again, lifting the betting record to 2-0 and the running profit to $11.50.
The biggest shape-shifters were the Warriors crushing Melbourne, the Titans smashing Parramatta, and the Tigers backing up the hype against Newcastle.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Cowboys were selected as the Round 6 value play
lines: The Cowboys call was built on form over fortress logic and it held up in a 35-31 derby win.
The TAB bet landed at $2.00, with the $5 stake returning $10.00 and locking in another clean charity win.
That keeps the process disciplined and pushes the running charity profit to $11.50 after two bets.

sections.title: Round 6 Fixtures

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who looked hot, who looked broken
lines: Hot: Tigers, Warriors, Cowboys, Roosters, Sea Eagles.
The biggest reset from this round is that Melbourne and Parramatta can no longer be treated as bounce-back defaults.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before publishing
lines: The Cowboys result validated the charity process, but the wider tipping card still showed how fragile favourite reads can be.
Warriors and Tigers deserve to be upgraded after what they did this round.
Storm, Eels and Dragons now need proof before they get any benefit of the doubt.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
